title: Did NGE replace anything for you
display_title: Did NGE replace anything for you
wikitext:
[]: Did NGE replace anything for you?             Quote Post by poetic_assassin » Fri Nov 18, 2005 1:07 pm Used to, Fooly Cooly was my favorite show. But now that NGE is within my grasp. It's not that I don't like Fooly Cooly anymore, but NGE is #1 now. So, did this show replace anything for you too?

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Fri Nov 18, 2005 1:42 pm Several gigabytes of harddrive space with Eva-related nonsense. Also quite a bit of money with DVDs and manga.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Nov 18, 2005 2:02 pm It filled the hobby gap that had prevented me from finally letting go of rolegaming. So to some extent, for me, it replaced D&D and its successors.

[]:             Quote Post by rb_182_ » Fri Nov 18, 2005 2:45 pm Yes as a matter of fact it replaced Video Games for me. I became to obsessed with Evangelion last year that I hadn't played any games for a few months so I unhooked by PS2 and since a year ago it's been sitting in my closet not being played. I used to play games like Final Fantasy and Xenosaga because I loved the deep storylines. but Evas storyline is far better in my opinion. plus eva then led me to watch tons of other anime so I spend all my time buying DVDs and shit now.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Fri Nov 18, 2005 2:47 pm Cowboy Bebob was my fav anime until I found NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Fri Nov 18, 2005 3:07 pm It replaced mah life completly. Mind body and soul.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Fri Nov 18, 2005 3:17 pm Evangelionfreak wrote: It replaced mah life completly. Mind body and soul.

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Fri Nov 18, 2005 3:19 pm Yeah I'm not allowed to go out side much because my skin burns too easy and mah mom doesn't let me out of the house.

[]:             Quote Post by Shnooks » Fri Nov 18, 2005 3:43 pm Gravitation. For the better, I assume. Atleast people dont have to hear me blabber in fangirl yaoi speak for 7 hours straight.

[]:             Quote Post by rb_182_ » Fri Nov 18, 2005 3:44 pm Evangelionfreak wrote: Yeah I'm not allowed to go out side much because my skin burns too easy and mah mom doesn't let me out of the house.

[]:             Quote Post by The Sandman » Fri Nov 18, 2005 3:46 pm Eva didn't change anything for me except for the most #1 series that I loved. Last year it was GTO. In 2003 that honor went to Ceres, Celestial Legend. Besides that nothing much.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Fri Nov 18, 2005 4:22 pm Eva introduced me to anime. Before that, I had spent more time on classic British sci-fi (old Doctor Who, The Prisoner) and Hammer Horror-type films than I do now. So, yes, it did change my intake of media. But "The Prisoner" is still one of the greatest series of all time. Doctor Who isn't bad either, but without anime I would probably be buying a lot more DW DVDs.

[]:             Quote Post by sheareraehs » Fri Nov 18, 2005 4:37 pm Several gigabytes of harddrive space with Eva-related nonsense. Also quite a bit of money with DVDs and manga.

[]:             Quote Post by TsubasaNoKoikokoro » Fri Nov 18, 2005 5:37 pm It replaced DNangel as my favorite anime. Then Madlax replaced DNangel with my second favorite. Then Elfen Lied replaced it has its third, ect. Currently, DNangel is nu,ber 7 on my list.

[]:             Quote Post by Eternal Yamcha » Fri Nov 18, 2005 5:59 pm Dragon Ball Z was number one for me... Then Evangelion came and trounced it savagely. I don't even remember how my top 5 goes any more, I really just stopped watching anime for the most part, even Evangelion. I wish I could get started again. Then again, I got plenty of other things I need to do to pre-occupy my time with. But I will have spent a fortune over my Evangelion obsession within the next couple years.

[]:             Quote Post by sachiel » Fri Nov 18, 2005 6:37 pm Same as Shnooks said, Gravitation. But I went from Eva to Gravitation to Eva again, because the first time I watched Evangelion, I was 11, and I didn't understand half the stuff I thought I did. o_O;

[]:             Quote Post by Digitalex » Fri Nov 18, 2005 8:31 pm It replaced (surpassed) Gundam and Macross as my number one all-time favorite series. It also got me into going back to Cons which in turn got me into even more anime and even more spending. It also led me to reading manga and surfing the internet for NGE related pics and works.

[]:             Quote Post by Six Winged Angel » Sat Nov 19, 2005 2:11 am Evangelion replaced all the good animes I had seen til that time, such as gundam wing, one piece, digimon 1st season and others....it's the best

[]:             Quote Post by Eternal Yamcha » Sat Nov 19, 2005 2:59 am It also replaced my girlfriend, car, house, pets, family and even replaced some of that good stuff that one guy on the corner told me about. Now I spend my days wandering the streets, philosophizing, carrying around $150 worth of Evangelion merchandise and screaming at people from my street corner... "Third impact will be soon on us!!!" Also... "We'll all return to the primordial soup!!!" And finally... "Hey buddy, you got a quarter?" "No" "I hope Unit 01 bites your **************************** head off!!!" What? Evangelion's good for me.

[]:             Quote Post by sachiel » Sat Nov 19, 2005 6:49 am Eternal Yamcha wrote: "Third impact will be soon on us!!!"
summary:
[]: Did NGE replace anything for you?             Quote Post by poetic_assassin » Fri Nov 18, 2005 1:07 pm Used to, Fooly Cooly was my favorite show. But now that NGE is within my grasp. It's not that I don't like Fooly Cooly anymore, but NGE is #1 now. So, did this show replace anything for you too?

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Fri Nov 18, 2005 1:42 pm Several
